title: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A U.S.-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese government took effect at midnight on April 17, 2026, halting six weeks of Israeli airstrikes and ground operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon that began March 2 following Hezbollah rocket fire into northern Israel. Hezbollah, not a direct party to the agreement, has signaled potential momentum toward talks via officials but has not formally endorsed it, while Israel intends to keep troops deployed and reserves the right to respond to violations. President Trump linked the pause to broader de-escalation, including possible U.S.-Iran negotiations, leaving trader consensus focused on compliance risks and prospects for extension before the truce expires around April 27 amid ongoing diplomatic pressures.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 17.2% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, closely trailed by France at 16.2%, driven by their top FIFA rankings and flawless UEFA qualification runs capped by dominant group wins. The March playoffs, which secured Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Türkiye, and Czechia via dramatic victories, finalized the 48-team field, revealing balanced groups that temper favorites—Spain faces Uruguay in a tough draw, while France contends with Senegal and Norway. England's 10.9% reflects inconsistent Nations League form, yet defending champions Argentina (8.8%) and Brazil (8.6%) lurk with CONMEBOL momentum, highlighting the expanded format's knockout volatility where upsets abound among 12 group winners, runners-up, and best third-placers advancing.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 99.3% implied probability of no change in the Federal Reserve's target range for the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, anchored by the March decision to hold steady at 3.50%-3.75% and subsequent minutes affirming consensus for just one rate cut later in 2026 amid geopolitical uncertainties and oil price spikes. March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—up from February's 2.4%—driven by energy surges, while unemployment held steady at 4.3%, reinforcing a patient policy stance as inflation remains above target without labor market distress. Realistic challenges include hotter-than-expected April data releases or escalating global risks prompting a hawkish pivot, though trader capital reflects skin-in-the-game confidence in continuity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 45.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions after clinching the Western Conference's No. 1 seed and NBA's best regular-season record around 64-18, securing home-court advantage throughout the playoffs starting April 19 with a first-round matchup versus the No. 8 Play-In winner. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play, alongside Chet Holmgren and additions like Alex Caruso, fuel their dominance amid a healthy roster entering postseason practices. San Antonio Spurs (14.8%) sit as No. 2 West seeds at 62-20, powered by Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way impact versus Portland in Round 1, while Boston Celtics (12.2%) and Denver Nuggets (9.5%) round out challengers with strong playoff pedigrees, Eastern contention, and Nikola Jokić's triple-double threat despite tougher paths.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27.7% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination in this wide-open field following the party's 2024 defeat, buoyed by his executive record as California governor, national fundraising prowess, and term-limited status positioning him for a full-time campaign. Recent appearances by Kamala Harris, who teased a potential run at the National Action Network convention last week, and others like Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg signal an intensifying shadow primary ahead of 2026 midterms that could elevate governors or senators with strong reelection performances. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.5% on progressive appeal to young voters, while Harris sits at 6.8% despite leading some national primary polls—highlighting market skepticism of her electability post-loss. Consolidation may hinge on early-state polling, donor commitments, and midterm battleground results.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner at 18.6%, narrowly ahead of California Governor Gavin Newsom at 17.2% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 10.4%, driven by Vance's incumbency advantage as the Republican heir apparent and a recent UMass Lowell poll showing him ahead 33%-30% in a head-to-head matchup. Newsom's rise reflects his status as the leading Democratic alternative amid post-2024 recalibrations, with both benefiting from early primary positioning before 2026 midterms test party strength in swing states. The tight contest underscores unproven paths-to-victory, lack of formal announcements, and balanced polling trends; separation could emerge from midterm results, President Trump's endorsement, foreign policy developments, or scandals shifting Electoral College math.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Direct US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad ended without agreement on April 11 after 21 hours of negotiations led by Vice President JD Vance, stalling progress toward a permanent deal despite a fragile two-week ceasefire holding since early April in the 2026 conflict. Pakistan's mediation persists, with a delegation in Tehran urging a second round before the truce expires, as Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz yesterday while facing a US naval blockade on its ports and demands for nuclear curbs including enriched uranium surrender and halted enrichment. Optimism from US officials contrasts with gaps on sanctions relief, reparations, and regional security, leaving traders focused on imminent diplomacy or escalation risks amid economic pressures.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures plunged over 10% to near $84 per barrel after Iran's Foreign Minister declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open, easing geopolitical supply disruption fears and stripping out a significant risk premium from prices. This drop to five-week lows reflects aggregated trader sentiment amid de-escalating Middle East tensions, with U.S. inventories unexpectedly falling 913,000 barrels to 463.8 million—1% above five-year averages—per the latest EIA report for the week ended April 10. Global demand dynamics, including China's economic recovery, remain key, alongside ample non-OPEC supply growth. Traders monitor this week's EIA update, potential OPEC+ output tweaks, and U.S. economic data for late-April price swings.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket places HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. slightly ahead at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, followed closely by Vice President J.D. Vance at 39.1% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 20.8%, reflecting a tightly contested early field shaped by their prominent roles in the Trump administration. RFK Jr.'s edge stems from his high-visibility congressional hearings this week, including clashes over vaccine policies and budget cuts that energize the MAHA movement and populist voters, while his cousin publicly affirmed his 2028 ambitions in March. Vance holds as MAGA heir apparent amid midterm focus, and Rubio gains from donor preferences favoring him over Vance. Separation could arise from 2026 midterm outcomes, Trump endorsements, or exploratory committee launches testing primary viability.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 10 - April 17, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 10 12:00 PM ET to April 17, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 100% implied probability to Elon Musk posting 300-319 times on X from April 10-17, 2026, anchored by real-time trackers confirming a total of 300 posts as the period hits 100% completion on April 17. This reflects his steady posting rhythm of about 39 per day over the prior seven days, with minimal activity today amid ongoing SpaceX developments and Tesla priorities that have curbed viral spikes. Consistent with recent weeks' patterns absent major political or company catalysts, the skin-in-the-game crowd sees little room for deviation. An upset would require an unforeseen controversy or announcement prompting 20+ last-minute posts, though historical frequency suggests low risk as the day closes.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 52.5% implied probability for Strait of Hormuz traffic returning to normal—defined as pre-crisis volumes of around 100 daily transits—by April 30, reflecting balanced sentiment amid fragile de-escalation signals. Iran's foreign minister declared the strait "completely open" to commercial vessels today during the ongoing Lebanon ceasefire, coupled with a slight post-April 8 rebound to 8 daily transits per AIS data, has nudged odds higher from recent lows. However, persistent U.S. blockade on Iranian ports, strict Iranian transit conditions, and 94% volume drop sustain No-side viability, as shipping operators remain risk-averse amid elevated insurance premiums and 150+ stranded vessels. Key swing factors include daily AIS transit acceleration, ceasefire extension beyond 10 days, and U.S.-Iran negotiation progress, with Brent crude reacting sharply lower on reopening hopes.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Counter-Strike: Spirit vs MOUZ (BO3) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 1 match between Spirit and MOUZ in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 17 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit win the match against MOUZ.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ win the match against Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 1 match between Spirit and MOUZ in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 17 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Spirit and MOUZ play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map Handicap: TS (-1.5) vs MOUZ (+1.5)
markets.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "MOUZ". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit holds a commanding head-to-head edge over MOUZ, winning 22 of 37 series including a 2-0 sweep at IEM Krakow in February, fueling trader consensus toward the #9-ranked Russians despite their shaky IEM Rio group stage path—2-0 over G2 and Liquid, 2-1 versus RED Canids, but a 0-2 loss to Falcons with dropped maps signaling inconsistency and donk's recent flu complaints. MOUZ, sitting at #8 with balanced map win rates, advanced via 2-0 over Aurora and 2-1 against Legacy but faltered 0-2 to FURIA, extending a two-month drought against tier-1 foes. In this BO3 quarterfinal, stylistic clashes on the map pool—Spirit's 75% Mirage success versus MOUZ's Dust2 strength—could decide advancement to semis, where the winner faces Falcons or Vitality.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' dominance in the early 2026 Formula 1 season, fueled by superior power unit performance and active aerodynamics under new regulations, has propelled teammates George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus, with Russell's 43.5% implied probability edging Antonelli's 30.3% despite the Italian's championship lead (72-63 points after three Grands Prix). Russell's Australian GP victory set a strong tone, but Antonelli's back-to-back China and Japan wins—capped by Suzuka pole over his teammate—have intensified their intra-team battle, drawing pundit warnings for Russell to match peak Hamilton aggression. Trailing Ferrari duo Leclerc (49 points) and Hamilton (41) face steeper paths amid Mercedes' early momentum and a long calendar ahead.

title: Indian Premier League: Gujarat Titans vs Kolkata Knight Riders
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Gujarat Titans and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for April 17 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Gujarat Titans vs Kolkata Knight Riders - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Gujarat Titans and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for 2026-04-17 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Gujarat Titans vs Kolkata Knight Riders - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Gujarat Titans and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for 2026-04-17 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gujarat Titans hold trader consensus edge over winless Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026's 25th match at batting paradise Narendra Modi Stadium, where chases dominate on the belter pitch no. 6 offering early pace bounce before slowing. GT sit sixth with two wins from four (recent WWLL form, NRR -0.029), boosted by captain Shubman Gill's 165 runs at 50+ average and Prasidh Krishna's purple cap-leading 10 wickets, while Rashid Khan boasts a stellar 6.9 economy. KKR languish 10th after four losses and one no-result (NRR -1.383), hampered by Matheesha Pathirana's absence and batting inconsistencies despite Sunil Narine's tight lines; head-to-head stands competitive at 3-3, but GT's home momentum and settled attack drive favoritism amid clear weather.

title: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing US-Iran negotiations over Iran's nuclear program remain stalled on the duration of any uranium enrichment suspension, with the United States demanding a 20-year pause and Iran countering with five years, as reported in talks that collapsed in Islamabad on April 15. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth demanded handover of Iran's roughly 970 pounds of highly enriched uranium stockpile on April 9, echoing President Trump's statements about working to "dig up and remove" it, but Tehran has refused. Earlier military options, including risky special forces raids briefed in late March, appear sidelined amid diplomatic efforts. IAEA reports confirm Iran's growing stockpile, fueling trader consensus implying low odds for US acquisition by May 31 absent a breakthrough, with potential for escalation or deal in coming weeks.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by violin virtuoso Linda Lampenius and pop star Pete Parkkonen has solidified trader consensus as the clear frontrunner at 37% implied probability following their dominant UMK national selection win in early March, buoyed by the track's infectious dance-pop hooks, live violin spectacle, and broad appeal to both juries and televoters. France's Monroe with "Regarde!" climbed to second on polished production and vocal prowess from an internal selection, while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard ("Før") surged via strong previews and Nordic momentum. Australia, Greece, and Israel trail amid competitive staging buzz, but Finland's lead persists amid completed national selections. With first rehearsals imminent ahead of Vienna semis on May 12/14, live performances could spark shifts in this skin-in-the-game sentiment.

title: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 17 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and JD Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and JD Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

markets.question: Game Handicap: NIP (-1.5) vs JD Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 17 at 8:10AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas wins 2 or more games than JD Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "JD Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Ascend, Ninjas in Pyjamas face JD Gaming in a pivotal BO3 series amid tight standings, with both teams at 3-2 records and jockeying for playoff positioning. JDG holds a commanding head-to-head edge, winning 8 of 9 recent encounters including an 86% dominance rate, bolstered by a 61% overall winrate and stronger recent form (3 wins in last 5 matches) against NIP's mixed 2-5 stretch. No major roster changes or substitutions reported, though NIP's midlaner Guwon has shown MVP flashes. Traders weigh JDG's macro control and map momentum in the current patch meta, while NIP's upset potential hinges on early-game aggression and draft adaptability in this double round-robin group stage.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Como's victory at 30.5% implied probability atop a tightly bunched market, reflecting their fifth-place Serie A standing, league-best defensive record (26 goals conceded), and unbeaten away streak spanning nine matches in 2026, bolstered by two clean-sheet wins over Sassuolo this season including a 2-0 league reversal. Sassuolo's 13.5% trails amid a lengthy absentee list—suspensions for Domenico Berardi and Josh Doig, injuries to Darryl Bakola, Filippo Romagna, Fali Cande, Edoardo Pieragnolo, and Daniel Boloca—forcing improvisation under Fabio Grosso at home, where they've claimed three of their last four wins. Recent setbacks like Sassuolo's 2-1 loss to Genoa (with Berardi's tunnel red) and Como's 4-3 collapse from 2-0 up against Inter keep dynamics competitive, elevating draw odds to 27.5% in this mid-table vs. top-five clash at Mapei Stadium.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's declaration today that the Strait of Hormuz is fully open to commercial traffic, tied to the new Israel-Lebanon ceasefire effective April 16 against Iran-backed Hezbollah, signals tentative de-escalation after nearly seven weeks of US-Israel airstrikes on Iranian nuclear, missile, and leadership targets since February 28. However, President Trump maintains the US naval blockade on Iranian ports—enforced without breaches since April 13—until a broader peace deal, with indirect Pakistan-mediated talks ongoing and Vice President Vance seeking a 20-year nuclear suspension. The initial US-Iran two-week truce expires April 22, leaving traders to watch for renewal, escalation via proxy actions, or diplomatic breakthroughs amid economic pressures from disrupted shipping.

title: LoL: ThunderTalk Gaming vs Oh My God (BO3) - LPL Group Nirvana
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between ThunderTalk Gaming and Oh My God in the LPL Group Nirvana, initially scheduled for April 17 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ThunderTalk Gaming" if ThunderTalk Gaming win the match against Oh My God.

This market will resolve to "Oh My God" if Oh My God win the match against ThunderTalk Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both ThunderTalk Gaming and Oh My God each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both ThunderTalk Gaming and Oh My God each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both ThunderTalk Gaming and Oh My God. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TT (-1.5) vs Oh My God (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between ThunderTalk Gaming and Oh My God in the LPL Group Nirvana, initially scheduled for April 17 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ThunderTalk Gaming" if ThunderTalk Gaming wins 2 or more games than Oh My God in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Oh My God". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: TT (-1.5) vs Oh My God (+1.5)

eventMetadata.context_description: ThunderTalk Gaming leads Group Nirvana standings at 2-0 series (4-1 games) after dominant 2-0 sweeps over LGD Gaming on April 10 and Ultra Prime on April 15, showcasing strong early-game aggression and macro execution in the LPL Split 2 group stage. Oh My God sits at 0-2 (2-4 games) following narrow losses to EDward Gaming and an earlier defeat, hampered by inconsistent bot lane performances and objective control issues. This BO3 matchup, pivotal for top-4 advancement to playoffs play-in amid single round-robin format, highlights TTG's momentum and rest advantage versus OMG's urgency to avoid bottom-2 elimination, with no reported roster changes or meta-shifting patches in the past week.

In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 17 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Desmond Bane records more than 3.5 assists during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Desmond Bane records 3.5 assists or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 17 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Hornets win, the market will resolve to "Hornets".
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Hornets" if the Hornets win the game by 4 or more points.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Hornets vs. Magic: O/U 218.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 17 at 7:30PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Hornets and Magic combine to score 219 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 219, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the NBA Play-In Tournament's decisive 8-vs-9 matchup at Kia Center, surging Charlotte Hornets visit the Orlando Magic with momentum from their 127-126 overtime thriller over Miami two nights ago, where LaMelo Ball's game-winning layup preserved their season. Hornets boast recent dominance in head-to-heads, including 130-111 and 124-97 routs of Orlando in March and January, fueled by Ball's playmaking and Brandon Miller's scoring. Magic, finishing 45-37 as the No. 8 seed, grapple with injuries—Jonathan Isaac (knee) questionable after a month out, Franz Wagner limping off versus Philadelphia with a leg issue, and Jett Howard sidelined (ankle)—testing home-court advantage amid late-season struggles. Moussa Diabate (hip) is Charlotte's lone questionable. Trader consensus weighs Hornets' form against Orlando's defensive potential if healthy.

title: 2026 Men's French Open Winner
description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner leads trader consensus at 45.5% implied probability for the 2026 French Open title after defeating Carlos Alcaraz 7-6(5), 6-3 in the Monte Carlo Masters final last week—his first clay Masters 1000 crown and biggest surface-specific achievement, extending his dominant 24-2 season record atop ATP rankings. Alcaraz holds 33.5% as the close challenger, bolstered by his two prior Roland Garros triumphs, 84% career clay win rate, and strong 22-3 2026 form despite the setback. Alexander Zverev's 4% reflects consistent clay contention but no recent breakthroughs, while Novak Djokovic's 2.9% stems from his Monte Carlo withdrawal amid injury concerns and limited prep time. Alcaraz's reported Madrid absence positions Sinner as likely top seed, with Barcelona and Madrid outcomes pivotal ahead of late-May Roland Garros.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori leads trader consensus at 66.5% implied probability to win Peru's presidency following her first-place finish in the April 12-13 general election first round, where fragmented support among 35 candidates limited top vote shares to around 17%. Logistical delays in ballot delivery extended voting and counting into this week, fueling fraud claims from third-place Rafael López Aliaga (9.5%) without evidence, while leftist congressman Roberto Sánchez Palomino surged to second (24.6%) in final tallies. Fujimori's frontrunner status reflects her established conservative base amid security concerns and prior near-victories, positioning her strongly for the June 7 runoff against Sánchez, with no candidate reaching 50% threshold.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US and Israeli airstrikes targeted military installations on Iran's Kharg Island oil export terminal on April 7, degrading air defenses and missile sites but sparing critical oil infrastructure, with Iranian state media confirming uninterrupted exports and full armed forces control as of mid-April. The island handles nearly 90% of Iran's crude shipments from the Persian Gulf, drawing U.S. threats of potential seizure from President Trump amid broader conflict escalation signals from Vice President Vance declaring negotiations over. Iran has layered defenses including mines and MANPADS since late March preparations for ground invasion risks. Traders weigh low-probability regime capitulation or U.S. amphibious operation against high retaliation risks in the Strait of Hormuz, with no verified loss of control despite unconfirmed claims; watch for diplomatic breakthroughs or further strikes.

title: Porsche Tennis Grand Prix: Karolina Muchova vs Coco Gauff
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Karolina Muchova and Coco Gauff in the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix, originally scheduled for April 17, 2026 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Karolina Muchova' if Karolina Muchova advances against Coco Gauff.

This market will resolve to 'Coco Gauff' if Coco Gauff advances against Karolina Muchova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Gauff" if Coco Gauff wins by 2 or more sets than Karolina Muchova, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Muchova."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Coco Gauff holds a perfect 6-0 head-to-head edge over Karolina Muchova, including a straight-sets semifinal win at the Miami Open last month, fueling trader consensus toward the No. 2 seed in this Porsche Tennis Grand Prix quarterfinal on indoor clay. Both players advanced convincingly yesterday: Muchova rallied from a first-set loss to dominate Elise Mertens 1-6, 6-3, 6-0 after routing qualifier Aliaksandra Sasnovich, extending her strong 2026 form (18-4 record, Doha WTA 1000 title); Gauff shook off a slow start to dispatch Liudmila Samsonova in straight sets following a first-round bye. Muchova's resurgent baseline game challenges Gauff's athleticism and return prowess on the slower surface, but the American's clay pedigree as reigning French Open champion and higher ranking tilt implied probabilities in her favor absent late withdrawals or injury reports.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the UEFA Champions League winner after their thrilling 4-3 second-leg quarterfinal victory over Real Madrid (6-4 aggregate), propelled by late strikes from Luis Díaz and Michael Olise, showcasing attacking depth and resilience. Arsenal advanced defensively with a 0-0 draw against Sporting CP (1-0 aggregate), while PSG dominated Liverpool and Atlético Madrid edged past Barcelona on penalties, setting up blockbuster semifinals: Bayern vs. PSG and Arsenal vs. Atlético. The tight clustering reflects evenly matched clashes—Bayern's momentum versus PSG's firepower, Arsenal's organization against Atlético's Simeone-style grit—with first legs looming April 28-29 amid full squad health and no major injuries reported.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from Datafolha, Quaest, Futura, and MDA conducted in early April 2026 show Senator Flávio Bolsonaro leading President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in first-round voting intentions by 2–9 points, with Flávio at 37–40% and Lula at 30–37%, while simulated runoffs remain statistically tied within margins of error. This trader consensus on Polymarket, pricing both top contenders near 40%, reflects a polarized contest where Lula's incumbency is offset by declining approval ratings around 44% amid economic headwinds, and Flávio gains from right-wing consolidation after withdrawals like Ratinho Júnior's. A Federal Police probe into Flávio for alleged defamation against Lula, announced April 15, introduces legal uncertainty. Party nominations by June and initial debates could create separation ahead of the October 4 first round.

title: Bitcoin price on April 17?
description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.
